params.text:
Understanding your duty of care

Your duty of care is the legal and moral responsibility you carry to keep the people you support safe and well while you are caring for them. It is not something you choose to take on — the moment you begin caring for someone, the duty applies automatically, and it stays with you throughout your role.

In practice, your duty of care means you must:

  • always act in the best interests of the people you support, and do nothing that could deliberately harm them
  • work within your job description, your training and your competence, and not attempt tasks you have not been trained for
  • report concerns, hazards and changes in a person's condition promptly
  • treat people with dignity and respect while keeping them safe from avoidable harm

Duty of care sits at the heart of safe practice. When every worker takes it seriously, risks are spotted earlier, mistakes are caught sooner, and people receive care that is both safe and respectful.

params.alt: Balancing duty of care with rights and choices

params.text:
Everyone shares the responsibility

Duty of care is not only about you as an individual. Your employer also owes a duty of care — to give you proper training, safe equipment, clear procedures and enough support to do your job well. In turn, you owe a duty of care to the people you support, to your colleagues and to yourself.

Meeting your duty of care does not mean wrapping people in cotton wool or taking away their independence. It means providing care that is competent, attentive and honest, and knowing when a situation is beyond what you can safely handle on your own.

params.question: Duty of care is optional — a care worker can decide whether or not to accept it.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. Duty of care is not optional. It is a legal obligation that applies automatically the moment you start providing care or support.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. Duty of care is a legal requirement that applies automatically as soon as you begin caring for someone. You cannot opt out of it.
